flashcards.definition: All organisms in an area plus nonliving physical components (soil, water, atmosphere); characterized by energy flow and nutrient cycling.
Biotic factors: living (plants, animals, microbes); Abiotic factors: nonliving (temperature, light, water, pH, nutrients, wind).
Energy levels in food chains: producers (plants), primary consumers (herbivores), secondary consumers (carnivores), tertiary consumers (top carnivores).
Only ~10% of energy is transferred between trophic levels; 90% is lost as heat and used in respiration; results in energy pyramid.
Shows total mass of organisms at each trophic level; also typically pyramidal due to energy losses, but can be inverted in aquatic systems.
Interconnected food chains showing multiple feeding relationships; more realistic than simple chains; shows energy transfer complexity.
Variety of species, genes, and ecosystems; increases ecosystem resilience, stability, and productivity; loss reduces ecosystem function.
Habitat: physical place where organism lives; Niche: role and function (what it eats, where it lives, how it reproduces).
Gradual ecosystem change over time; primary (bare rock to forest), secondary (recovery after disturbance); leads to climax community.
Maximum population size environment can sustain; determined by available resources; exceeded when population crashes.
